article_title: Tesla Becomes Intel Foundry's First Major Customer for 14A as Musk Commits Terafab to Unproven Process
summary: Substantively excellent Tesla-Intel 14A piece — Intel newsroom + Fortune + Tom's Hardware quotes all verify verbatim, Musk PC Gamer quote verbatim — but two issues: (1) Yahoo Finance snapshot is a cookie wall, no readable body, so the Bernstein $5-13T capex / 2028-2029 schedule / two Austin facilities claims attributed to it cannot be verified through the snapshot; (2) '18A yields improving ~7% per month' is not in the cited Tom's Hardware 18A piece.

findings.message: Yahoo Finance snapshot is a cookie/consent wall, not the article body — multiple claims attributed to it are unverifiable through this snapshot
findings.details: source-2.html (Yahoo Finance) is ~2,700 chars of Italian cookie consent text with no article body. The 3 claims attributed to Yahoo / Foreign Policy Journal — Bernstein $5-13 trillion cumulative capex, Intel 14A risk production 2028 / volume ramp 2029, two distinct Austin facilities (vehicles+humanoids vs space-based data centers) — cannot be verified from this snapshot. Either re-source the claims from a different outlet (e.g., the Foreign Policy Journal original or another financial outlet) or remove.

findings.message: Unsourced: '18A is improving by roughly 7 percent per month'
findings.details: Tom's Hardware source-4 does not contain '7%', 'per month', or any monthly yield-improvement rate. Zinsner is quoted only qualitatively ('by the end of next year we will probably be in that space'). The 7%/month figure is not in this snapshot. Either source separately or drop the figure (the 'industry-standard yields not until 2027' framing IS verbatim and can stand on its own).

findings.message: Source redirected to different domain: consent.yahoo.com
Source fetched via Archive.org fallback (original returned 200)
Source fetched via Archive.org fallback (original returned 200)

content_preview.title: Tesla Becomes Intel Foundry's First Major Customer for 14A as Musk Commits Terafab to Unproven Process
content_preview.summary: Elon Musk picked Intel's 14A process for Tesla's Terafab AI chip complex, handing the struggling Intel Foundry its first marquee external win for a node that does not enter risk production until 2028.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Tesla has committed its planned Terafab semiconductor complex in Austin to Intel's forthcoming 14A process node, making the electric-vehicle and robotics maker the first publicly identified marquee customer for Intel Foundry's most aggressive technology bet. CEO Elon Musk disclosed the decision on Tesla's April 22 earnings call, and Intel CEO Lip-Bu Tan confirmed the relationship a day later when Intel reported first-quarter results, saying he could "think of no better partner than ...

recommendations: Consider adding trusted domains to config/source_allowlist.txt
Re-source the Yahoo-attributed claims (Bernstein $5-13T capex, 2028-2029 schedule, two Austin facilities) since the snapshot contains only a cookie wall.
Drop or re-source the '~7% per month' 18A yield improvement figure.

editor_notes.content_quality: Substantively excellent Hardware News piece (~1294 words; over the News 1200-word ceiling but acceptable). Strong structure (Overview / What We Know / Terafab Scale / What Intel Gets / What We Don't Know / Analysis), good 'mutual dependency' framing.
editor_notes.source_verification: Read all 6 snapshots. Intel newsroom verifies Fab 34 / $14.2B / Apollo / 2024 minority stake unwound. Fortune verifies Q1 $13.6B / +7% / -2% expectation / Q2 $13.8-14.8B / +22% AH / Tan 'broad relationship' framing / Tan 'great progress' yield/cycle quote. Tom's Hardware (3) verifies all 3 Tan/Musk quotes verbatim. Tom's Hardware (4) verifies '2027 industry-standard yields' but NOT '7% per month'. PC Gamer Musk quote verbatim. Yahoo Finance snapshot is a cookie wall — Bernstein/2028-2029/two-facilities claims unverifiable through this snapshot.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All Intel/Fortune/Tom's Hardware/PC Gamer claims verify. Two issues: Yahoo snapshot unusable, '7% per month' unsourced.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral, analytical. The 'announcement that benefits each at low immediate cost' framing is well-grounded in what the source actually shows.
editor_notes.originality: New topic. Internal cross-references to Tesla Terafab and AI5 articles valid.
concerns: Yahoo Finance snapshot is unusable; multiple claims attributed to it unverifiable.
'7% per month' figure not in cited Tom's Hardware piece.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Excellent piece on a real strategic announcement. The Yahoo snapshot issue is a tooling problem (cookie wall captured instead of body); the bot can either find a different source for those specific claims or remove them. Two fixes and ready for re-review.
